core_mechanism: non-local encoding of state in a topologically degenerate subspace, transformed by a representation of the braid group acting on worldlines, so that the encoded information depends only on the homotopy class of the exchange paths and is therefore invariant under local perturbations
brief_reason: The abstract stores information in the topological degeneracy of multi-quasiparticle states and performs operations by braiding particles (non-Abelian braiding statistics), with fault-tolerance arising precisely from the non-local encoding that makes the state immune to local perturbations.
